headline: Langkawi Geopark Status Under UNESCO Review After Overdevelopment
context: UNESCO placed Langkawi Global Geopark under review in January 2026 following concerns that 14 new resort developments encroach on 3 of 7 designated geosites.

cards.big: Langkawi has been a UNESCO Global Geopark since 2007.
cards.sub: UNESCO's review — the first for any Malaysian site — was triggered by 14 resort projects within designated geosite boundaries.

cards.big: LADA approved 14 developments within geosite buffer zones since 2020.
cards.sub: UNESCO's guidelines require prior consultation. LADA did not notify UNESCO for any of the 14.

cards.big: Geopark status contributes an estimated RM450M annually to Langkawi tourism.
cards.sub: Loss of status would remove Langkawi from a global network of 195 geoparks attracting 120M visitors.

cards.big: 3 geosites contain Cambrian-era fossils dating to 500 million years ago.
cards.sub: Construction at Machinchang has already damaged fossil-bearing rock strata that cannot be restored.

cards.big: Building resorts on 500-million-year-old geosites to attract tourists who come for the geosites is self-defeating.
Langkawi's development authority must choose: short-term resort revenue or long-term geopark status. The current path risks losing both the geology and the tourists.
